column8: The Multimedia Art Museum is Moscow's museum for the photographic arts and art technology. It is a_______________new museum to the city, opening its doors in 2010. (RELATIVE)
column9: The museum operates alongside the Moscow House of Photography, which showcases the work of Russian photographers and other___________from past and contemporary ears. (ART)
column10: Both museums support modern photographers and the________________of the field by collaborating with leading European art institutions. (ADVANCE)
column11: The Multimedia Art Museum provides______________programmes for artists and for the general public, in an effort to encourage audiences to interact with exhibits. (EDUCATE)
column12: The museum also has special programmes aimed at helping______________children take part in photography. The crators of these programmes believe that despite their handicaps, these children have a unique perspective on life that adds a special creativity to their work. (ABLE)
column13: ________________are held in which photographers can submit collections of photos and receive monetary awards and a spot in the museum's permanent collection. The theme is life in the Russian capital and collections can focus on people, architecture or everyday events. (COMPETE)
column14: For a country so far north, the UK has mild weather in comparison to other countries that share similar latitudes. This is due to warm currents of water that are____________pushed up from the central Atlantic Ocean and around the British Isles. (CONTINUAL)
column15: And, because it is located in a northern part of the globe and benefits from______________breezes, it never really gets too hot, either. In fact, the warmest UK temperature on record is 38.5 degrees Celsius, recorded in Kent, England. (COAST)
column16: This makes for an interesting climate, as the UK is sandwiched between warm and cool air masses. As a result, the skies can be quite cloudy and there is often an____________of direct sunlight throughout the year. (ABSENT)
column17: Still, the milder weather, abundant rainfall, and fairly gentle landscape allow for a grat deal of livestock farming and crop____________ . (PRODUCE)
column18: While snow falls in Scotland and______________areas on around 40-50 days per year, the south west of England recieves snow on only around 10 days annually. (MOUNTAIN)
column19: And although it does get warm in the summer, a heatwave that lasts mere than a few days is fairly___________ . (LIKELY)
